channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Ex-central banker to replace Trudeau as Canada's prime minister after winning Liberal Party vote
item.description: Former central banker Mark Carney will become Canada’s next prime minister after the governing Liberal Party elected him its leader as the country deals with U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade war and annexation threat, and a federal election looms.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump downplays business concerns about uncertainty from his tariffs and prospect of higher prices
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump is dismissing business concerns over the uncertainty caused by his planned tariffs on a range of American trading partners and the prospect of higher prices, and isn't ruling out the possibility of a recession this year.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: D.C.'s Black Lives Matter mural will be erased. Look back at the iconic street painting
item.description: Over the past five years, Washington, D.C.'s iconic Black Lives Matter street painting has served as a powerful symbol of activism and a gathering place for joy and resistance.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: Fetterman maverick persona doesn't translate as senator helps block trans sports bill
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman's political statements and his blunt nature haven't necessarily translated to his voting record, which show he tends to vote with his party on key issues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weeks after Epstein file fallout, a new deadline looms in the release of the RFK and MLK files
item.description: The deadline to submit a release plan for the declassification of the RFK and MLK files comes just a few weeks after the DOJ's fallout over its release of Epstein-related files.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's speech to Congress ends notion that Dems are the 'compassionate' party after 92 years: expert
item.description: Former speechwriters for previous presidential administrations weighed in on President Donald Trump’s expansive speech before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top US health agency makes $25,000 buyout offer to most of its employees
item.description: Most of the 80,000 federal workers responsible for researching diseases, inspecting food and administering Medicare and Medicaid under the auspices of the Health and Human Services Department were emailed an offer to leave their job for as much as a $25,000 payment.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk and DOGE try to slash government by cutting out those who answer to voters
item.description: Conservatives have long talked about cutting government, but Republicans in Congress have shied away from approving deep reductions because of fear of how voters would react.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump's transactional approach to diplomacy is a driving force on the world stage
item.description: Donald Trump's style of diplomacy is a whole lot like a high-stakes business deal, and his No. 1 goal is to come out of the transaction on top.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Vance ramps up his clout in Trump world
item.description: Vice President Vance’s clout is on the rise, taking on the role of pot stirrer at the White House and on the world stage.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-district Democrats face risky vote on GOP spending bill
item.description: House Democrats in districts carried by President Trump are confronting a potentially risky vote next week when GOP leaders are expected to put their partisan government funding bill on the floor.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers consider allowing heavier ATVs
item.description: The ATV industry says consumer preferences are shifting toward larger side-by-side vehicles. Some environmental groups question whether heavier vehicles could cause more trail damage.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: South Dakota law raises questions about future of massive Midwest pipeline
item.description: SIOUX FALLS, S.D. (AP) — A new law in South Dakota prohibiting the use of eminent domain to acquire land for carbon capture pipelines raises questions about the viability of a proposed 2,500 mile (4,023-kilometer) project snaking through five Midwest states.
item.dc:creator: SARAH RAZA and JACK DURA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tribes and Native American students sue over Bureau of Indian Education firings
item.description: NORMAN, Okla. (AP) — Three tribal nations and five Native American students say in a lawsuit that the Trump administration has failed its legal obligations to tribes when it cut jobs at Bureau of Indian Education schools.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs already costing border money, jobs
item.description: The Trump administration’s on-again, off-again tariffs on Mexico and Canada already are costing businesses money and residents jobs in border cities like El Paso and Juarez.
item.dc:creator: Jesus Baltazar, NewsNation

item.title: Top Democrats ask Rubio for answers on now-canceled $400 million Tesla plan
item.description: A letter from two House Democrats presses Rubio for details about who approved an effort to try to use hundreds of millions of dollars of taxpayer money on armored electric vehicles from Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration cancels $400M in grants and contracts with Columbia University
item.description: Multiple federal agencies announced Friday that they're canceling grants and contracts because of what the government describes as the Ivy League school’s lackluster efforts to squelch antisemitism on campus. The U of M is one of five colleges under new federal antisemitism investigations.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: A downbeat Minnesota budget forecast initiates lawmaker blame game
item.description: Minnesota lawmakers received an economic budget forecast that shows less wiggle room as they write a new budget and an even larger looming deficit in the future. Gov. Tim Walz and DFL leaders blamed President Donald Trump while Republican leaders said Democrats need to look in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Social Security offices in NY set to close amid DOGE budget cuts
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Several Social Security offices in New York will be closing as the Department of Government Efficiency continues to cut federal spending. The closures come after the Social Security Administration said it found over $800 million in savings for the 2025 fiscal year.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump changes course and delays some tariffs on Mexico and Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Thursday postponed 25% tariffs on many imports from Mexico and some imports from Canada for a month amid widespread fears of the economic fallout from a broader trade war.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER RUGABER, JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Aces could join Las Vegas teams with their own specialty license plates
item.description: Las Vegas Aces fans might get a chance to show their support as the state considers issuing a specialty license plate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tim Scott unveils bill targeting debanking
item.description: Debanking — the closure of accounts that banks consider risky, often with little notice or explanation — has become a key issue for Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
